channel.title: Comments for page "Critter Profile: Genghis!"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Critter Profile: Genghis!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I very unironically love this band, lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so i guess you could say

that the corn he eats

is Genghis' Corn

i will see myself out

item.title: (no title)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Think about it that way: if you didn't knew Dschinghis Khan, you would probably just assume a made-up name or whatever and don't even pay attention to this.

By the way, I think I'll take this cue and hear them a little before I leave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed, although I do find it a little strange to like Dschinghis Khan that much. I mean, it is a good band with a couple very good songs, but favorite?

item.title: Re: Another WWS GoI Format! Woot!
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure about Tim Wilson not writing the critter profiles.

As far as I got it, WWS isn't that big a thing. Even with their pretty decent ability to get their hands on anomalies, they are still mostly the Non-profit, NGO they purport to be, and most of their actual work is still on normal animals. Then you get that most anomalies are tagged and released for surveillance, and those don't get Profiles, others are redeemed at least partially to the Foundation, and since they get proper SCP archives they don't get Profiles, either. The actual frequency of Critter Profiles being made is probably small.

Writing those Critter Profiles is a lot easier in-universe than out-of-universe, because it is just jotting down facts and things after all was said and done. The work that really is too much for Wilson to be doing personally all the time isn't done by him, it is to search, gather and tend to the several non-human guests and residents of the WWS. At this stage, writing the Profiles seems to me like the work that Wilson would actually want to do personally the most, because is above all his opportunity to be in touch with everything happening at WWS.

On the classification, this thing actually isn't too much more destructive than a normal locust swarm. Indeed it went undetected for quite some time, and was seemingly on the verge of annihilation when WWS found them. Even considering that normal locusts can lay as many as 150 eggs in the course of a month or so of reproductive time, this one doubling at an unspecified rate (but I think once a day is a reasonably safe assumption) isn't so much quicker than a common locust swarm to reproduce.

It is, indeed, a problem, and the WWS screwed up here, but I don't think that was so far-fetched. It was a reasonable mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Including how Genghis got his name was a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After we got the little fellow back, Karl asked me if he could name him after his favorite band, which I really had no objections to (I do the same myself sometimes). When he started calling the cute guy "Dschinghis Khan"

The Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon is at play again for me.

item.title: Another WWS GoI Format! Woot!
item.content:encoded:
Alright, before I say anything, my verdict is an upvote. However, I have some likes and dislikes, as follows:

Some Likes & Dislikes
Or What Some Would Call A Normal Critique

Let's start with things I liked.

Our experts (yes, we've got some) […]

Liked this line. Liked calling the SCP Foundation the Supervisors, seeing as I have had them call them that and some other writers for WWS have not picked up on it (not that I can complain, it's a GoI and people can do whatever they want with them, just saying I like seeing my terminology used).

The tone of the article is just about on point for WWS. I like that their undying kindness towards animals extends even to insects (as I had pictured them), and while I think that it would be a little weird for WWS to have too many greenhouses, it makes sense that they would have a few, so I enjoyed that detail. Gave insight into what their HQ and getup looks like.

Now, things that I didn't like. Don't worry, you did nothing terrible, seeing as I upvoted, but there were enough things that I felt I should make a short little list.

I think it's a bit weird to imply that Tim Wilson writes all the Critter Profiles. You would imagine that WWS would have a lot of them, and Tim Wilson is the head of the organization, ambassador to the SCP Foundation, manages their assets, puts on fundraisers, brings everyone coffee… you know, he has plenty of jobs. It's kind of odd to think he'd be in charge of writing Critter Profiles. It would be like implying that The Administrator writes all the SCPs. I feel the Administrator just wouldn't have time, with all the shit they need to be doing.

The mention of his name also feels a little forced. "I, Tim Wilson…" Like you just wanted to point out that you're using the character. There are other established WWS characters throughout the articles, and it'd be fine if you made some up. I just think this use of Tim Wilson as a character doesn't make total sense to me.

The effects of this little dude seem pretty clearly destructive, and it's a bug. I'm wondering why it's not Keter in the eyes of the Supervisors (which would put it under SCP jurisdiction)? It rides the line, so I suppose it could be Euclid, but it's tottering between the two classifications. I might mention some heavier than normal supervision by the Supervisors, to address this point? Not sure exactly when you would mention that, but I feel that it is important enough to be addressed.

Alright, and that was that. Good locusts of the apocalypse. Good critter profile. I approve… Not that you need my approval to write WWS, or that my opinion on this matters more than anyone else's, just, y'know. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoy Wilson's Wildlife from the few pages I've seen them in, and this article is no exception. Ghengis is so cute, it's gonna suck when he devours the world. +1
